title: phyllostegia decemiflorifer
intro: Phyllostegia decemiflorifer, also known as Hawaiian boxwood or ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It belongs to the Lamiaceae family and has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits like reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Traditional herbal preparations of Phyllostegia decemiflorifer include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken orally for medicinal purposes.
